name: Super Chunky Cookies
recipeIngredient: 4 ounces Bittersweet chocolate, chopped into 1/2-inch chunks
3/4 cup English toffee bits (such as Hershey's Skor)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Position one rack in the top third and another in the bottom third of the oven.
2. In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda, and salt until well blended.
3. In a heavy-duty electric mixer fitted with the paddle attachment, beat butter for 30 seconds at medium speed until creamy. Add sugars and continue beating for 3 to 4 minutes until light in texture and color. Scrape down sides of bowl with a rubber spatula.
4. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Beat in vanilla extract.
5. At low speed, beat in flour mixture three times, scraping down sides of bowl after each addition. Stir in all chocolate chips, bittersweet chocolate, and white chocolate. Stir in pecans and toffee bits. The dough will be very stiff.
6. Drop dough by slightly rounded tablespoonfuls onto ungreased baking sheets, leaving about 1 1/2 inches between cookies. Refrigerate remaining dough. Bake for 11 to 13 minutes, until set and edges lightly browned; switch positions of baking sheets halfway through baking time for even browning. Cool cookies on baking sheets on wire racks for 1 to 2 minutes, then transfer to wire racks to cool completely.
7. Repeat with remaining dough. Cookies can be stored in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 5 days or frozen for up to 1 month.
